pi_items.verified_value: Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties
pi_items.source: United Nations Official Documents, legal.un.org, treaties.un.org, multiple legal databases (vLex, Refworld, Cornell Law, OAS)
Multiple academic and legal sources confirm VCLT as standard abbreviation for Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties
Official UN documents confirm convention was concluded at Vienna on 23 May 1969 (UN iLibrary, treaties.un.org, legal.un.org)
Official UN Treaty Series publication database and multiple legal references confirm publication in UNTS
Multiple authoritative sources confirm Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties is published in UNTS volume 1155 (treaties.un.org, vLex, SCIRP, OAS, UN iLibrary)
Multiple authoritative sources confirm Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties appears at page 331 in UNTS volume 1155 (vLex, OAS, SCIRP, Oxford Law, Refworld)

Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties is a multilateral international treaty adopted by the United Nations (confirmed by UN, Wikipedia, Britannica, Cornell Law, and multiple international law sources)

privileged_info_summary: The Vienna Convention on the Law of Treaties is a real, publicly documented international treaty concluded on 23 May 1969, published in United Nations Treaty Series volume 1155, page 331. It is widely known by the abbreviation VCLT and serves as the foundational instrument governing how international treaties are drafted, interpreted, and amended. All metadata values in the task correctly represent this actual, publicly available legal document.
